title: Capsella jeniseiensis
taxonomy: Capsella jeniseiensis is classified in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It belongs to the genus Capsella, which is a group of small annual weeds. This classification places Capsella jeniseiensis among other species such as Capsella bursa-pastoris, the shepherd's purse. The Brassicaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that includes many economically important crops and weeds. Capsella jeniseiensis is a member of the order Brassicales, which is a subset of the larger group of flowering plants, the angiosperms.

intro: Capsella jeniseiensis is a flowering plant species within the Brassicaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which are believed to provide relief from coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Capsella jeniseiensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Capsella jeniseiensis is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as tinctures and syrups.
